title: Headache: SNNOOP-10 red flags
key_recommendations: Thunderclap headache (max severity within minutes) requires urgent non-contrast CT head; if CT negative within 6 hours of onset, sensitivity for SAH approaches 100%; LP if CT negative and onset >6 hours.
Fever + neck stiffness + headache: bacterial or viral meningitis - LP after CT if focal deficits or immunocompromised.
First or worst headache in patient >50 always warrants imaging.
Pattern change in chronic headache patient warrants reassessment.
OCP use, smoking, and dehydration raise risk of cerebral venous sinus thrombosis.
